evals.prompt: I want to build a tool that helps me track my reading habits
evals.expected_output: A comprehensive interview that surfaces non-obvious questions about what 'tracking' means, the user's real motivation, what reading formats are included, and produces a clear spec
expectations: Questions are clearly delineated with one concept per question, not walls of text
At least 2 questions surface topics the initial prompt didn't mention (e.g., social features, data portability, reading formats beyond books)
Later questions reference or build on earlier answers
At least 1 question challenges an assumption or pushes back on the idea
The interview explicitly addresses what is NOT in scope
Final spec has clear sections including problem, scope, and success criteria
Key decisions are documented with rationale, not just listed
Spec identifies risks or open questions that remain unresolved

evals.prompt: I'm thinking about building a local-first note-taking app that syncs across devices without a central server
evals.expected_output: An interview that digs into distributed systems challenges (conflict resolution, peer discovery, offline-first), UX tradeoffs, and the tension between simplicity and sync reliability
expectations: Questions are clearly delineated with one concept per question, not walls of text
At least 2 questions surface topics the initial prompt didn't mention (e.g., conflict resolution, peer discovery, encryption)
Later questions reference or build on earlier answers
At least 1 question challenges an assumption or pushes back on the idea
The interview explicitly addresses what is NOT in scope
Final spec has clear sections including problem, scope, and success criteria
Key decisions are documented with rationale, not just listed
Spec identifies risks or open questions that remain unresolved

evals.prompt: I want to start a weekly newsletter about AI tools for non-technical managers
evals.expected_output: An interview that adapts to a non-software context, exploring audience specificity, voice/tone, distribution strategy, sustainability, and content differentiation
expectations: Questions are clearly delineated with one concept per question, not walls of text
At least 2 questions surface topics the initial prompt didn't mention (e.g., monetization, content pipeline, differentiation from existing newsletters)
Later questions reference or build on earlier answers
At least 1 question challenges an assumption or pushes back on the idea
The interview explicitly addresses what is NOT in scope
Final spec has clear sections including problem, scope, and success criteria
Key decisions are documented with rationale, not just listed
